source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Use simple formulae, express missing-number problems algebraically, find pairs of numbers that satisfy an equation with two unknowns, and enumerate possibilities of combinations of two variables.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This transition-focused pack develops one connected idea: a symbol can stand for an unknown value or a general quantity while equality remains balanced.

objective.child_goal: I can use symbols for unknowns and keep equations true.
objective.statement: Represent unknown quantities with symbols, evaluate simple formulae and reason about values that make equations true.
misconceptions: Treats a letter as a label or object rather than a number that can vary.
Reads the equals sign as an instruction to calculate instead of a statement that two expressions have the same value.
Substitutes into only part of a formula or ignores the order of operations.
Finds one valid pair for an equation with two unknowns and assumes it is the only solution.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A symbol, often a letter, that can stand for a number.
vocabulary.audio_script: A variable is a symbol whose value can change.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A letter tile connected to several possible number cards.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A value that has not been found yet.
vocabulary.audio_script: An unknown is the number we need to work out.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A covered number box inside a true equation.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A rule written with numbers, symbols and operations.
vocabulary.audio_script: A formula is a general calculation rule.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Input cards flowing through a reusable rule machine.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Replace a symbol with its given value.
vocabulary.audio_script: To substitute, put the given number wherever the symbol appears.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A number tile replacing every matching variable tile.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Having the same value, even when written differently.
vocabulary.audio_script: Equivalent expressions have the same value.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A balance holding two differently written expressions level.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Retrieve inverse-operation reasoning and connect a box with an unknown value.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The box hides a number. Use the relationship on both sides to find it.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A balanced equation with one covered number card.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: One rule can work for many inputs: output equals three times n plus two.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce a variable as a general number in a reusable rule.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The letter n can take different number values, but the rule stays the same.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Several input cards pass through the same 3n + 2 function machine.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: For c = 5, evaluate 4c + 3 by replacing c with 5.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model complete substitution and operation order.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Replace c with five: four times five, then add three, gives twenty-three.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The 5 tile replaces c before the multiplication and addition stages light in order.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build both sides of 3n + 2 = 20, then find n without tipping the balance.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect inverse operations with equality and symbolic unknowns.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Keep both sides equal. Undo plus two, then undo times three.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A balance model removes two from both sides and partitions the remaining eighteen into three equal groups.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which pairs make a + b = 12 true?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Diagnose whether the learner recognises multiple valid solutions.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Test each pair by checking whether the two values total twelve.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Coordinate-like pair cards slot into an equation tester.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach the coach why 7 + 5 = 9 + 3 is true even though neither side is just an answer.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Secure a relational understanding of the equals sign before Year 7 algebra.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Explain what the equals sign says about the value on each side.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The coach points at two expression cards on a level balance.

manipulatives.purpose: Link missing-number arithmetic, function rules, substitution and equality through one consistent symbolic model.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners replace variables with values, run function machines, remove equal amounts from both sides and test ordered solution pairs.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Provide keyboard and switch access, text descriptions of balance state, colour-independent variable labels, optional concrete number tiles and a no-drag tap-to-place mode for SEND learners.

manipulatives.purpose: Show that a formula is a reusable rule mapping different inputs to outputs.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners choose or build operation stages, predict outputs and reverse a machine to recover an input.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Offer linear step cards, spoken rule replay, reduced symbol density and static before/after panels.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave missing-number arithmetic, formula evaluation and solution-pair checks only after the learner distinguishes an unknown from a varying quantity.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Translate familiar missing-number structures into equations containing a letter.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: bar or box model + missing-number sentence + equivalent letter equation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep arithmetic accessible so the assessed demand is representation, not calculation load.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Evaluate one- and two-operation formulae across varied input values.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: formula ax plus or minus b + input value + staged output choices
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include repeated-variable cases only in secure and stretch bands.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Reason about equivalence and solve one-step or two-step equations using balance-preserving moves.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: equivalent expressions or simple equation + legal move choices + balance model
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Distractors must diagnose operation reversal or one-sided changes.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Find and systematically enumerate integer pairs satisfying a two-unknown equation.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: equation ax plus by equals c + candidate pairs or constrained table
variant_blueprints.review_notes: State domains clearly and avoid implying infinitely many answers when only non-negative integers are intended.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain substitution, equality and unknown-value reasoning through spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review scheduler selects one short item by weakest misconception and prior format
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use calm, untimed retrieval and alternate symbolic prompts with visual models.

body.prompt: A shape pattern has T = 4n + 1 tiles in figure n. How many tiles are in figure 6?

hints: Replace n with 6 everywhere it appears.
Work out the value of the left side.
What must be added to 20 to make the same value?
question_variants.explanation: The left side equals 25, so 20 + n must also equal 25. Therefore n = 5.

body.prompt: Sam has p stickers. Jo has 8 more stickers than Sam. Build an expression for Jo's stickers.

hints: '8 more' means add 8, not multiply by 8.
question_variants.explanation: Jo has Sam's p stickers plus 8 more, so the expression is p + 8.

body.prompt: For non-negative whole numbers, which list gives every pair (a, b) with a < b that satisfies a + b = 8?

hints: Start with a = 0 and increase a systematically.
Stop before a and b become equal because the condition says a < b.
question_variants.explanation: The four listed pairs total 8 and meet a < b. The pair (4, 4) is excluded because the values are equal.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats a letter as a label or object rather than a number that can vary.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner interprets 4n as 'four and n' or chooses an expression based on the first letter of an object.
repair_sequence: Replace a missing-number box with a letter while keeping the same equation.
Show the letter taking three different number values in the same rule.
Ask the learner to state what number the letter represents in each case.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner translates a new missing-number problem into a correct letter expression and evaluates it for two values.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Reads the equals sign as an instruction to calculate instead of a statement that two expressions have the same value.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner rejects a true equation with expressions on both sides or changes only one side of a balance.
repair_sequence: Evaluate both sides of a true numerical equation.
Place the expressions on a physical or visual balance.
Make the same inverse-operation move on both sides.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner completes and explains a non-standard equation such as 16 + 9 = 20 + n.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Substitutes into only part of a formula or ignores the order of operations.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner adds before multiplying or leaves one occurrence of the variable unreplaced.
repair_sequence: Highlight every occurrence of the variable.
Replace each highlighted symbol with the given value.
Use operation-stage cards to calculate in the correct order.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner evaluates a two-operation formula and narrates the substitution and operation order.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Finds one valid pair for an equation with two unknowns and assumes it is the only solution.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner stops after the first pair or lists pairs without a systematic pattern.
repair_sequence: Fix the first variable at the smallest allowed value.
Find the matching second value and record the pair in a table.
Increase the first variable systematically until the stated condition ends the search.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner enumerates all pairs in a new bounded whole-number equation without duplicates or omissions.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a calm workbench, steady highlights and no tipping or alarm effects for incorrect equations.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace moving balance pans and travelling tiles with static before-and-after states.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read symbols as complete mathematical phrases and replay each operation stage separately.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Pair algebraic notation with bar models, plain-language rules and uncluttered symbol cards.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Reveal one stage at a time: represent, substitute, calculate, then check equality.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited working time, an operation-order strip and persistent display of the given variable value.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Normalise unknowns as familiar missing-number problems and credit correct representation separately from arithmetic accuracy.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach variable, unknown, formula, substitute, expression and equals with visual examples and sentence frames.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires translating missing-number situations, evaluating simple one- and two-operation formulae and finding an unknown in an equivalent equation; secure requires explaining equality and systematically finding all bounded solution pairs.
evidence.parent_summary: Can use letters for unknown numbers, follow simple formulae and keep both sides of an equation equal.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Represents quantities symbolically, substitutes accurately, preserves operation order, explains the equals sign relationally and enumerates constrained solution pairs.
next_actions: Interleave formulae from perimeter, sequences and conversion contexts.
Route repeated equality errors to balance-model intervention before symbolic rearrangement.
Bridge to Year 7 expression language only after variable and equality meanings are secure.

qa.notes: Review-status Year 6 algebra-readiness sample. SEND review must test symbol density, audio rendering of notation, keyboard/switch operation and concrete-to-symbol scaffolds before promotion.
